task: v147 preference local answer formatting first stage

module_location: src/jarvis_lite/preferences.py already owns saved preferences, confirmation records, undo and the active-confirmation matching contract.
src/jarvis_lite/agent.py handles ordinary input through InnerBrain, local data answer, LLM fallback, then long-term memory fallback.
src/jarvis_lite/knowledge.py formats deterministic data directory answers through answer_from_matches().
src/jarvis_lite/llm.py builds provider instructions; preference management commands are intentionally excluded.
current_behavior: 0.141.0 exposes the latest valid preference confirmation only to ordinary LLM fallback context and /llm-context-preview.
_answer_from_data() returns answer_from_matches(matches) directly, so local knowledge answers currently do not show a preference audit note.
The long-term memory fallback returns a fixed profile summary string directly, so it currently does not show a preference audit note.
_active_preference_application_for_reply() already invalidates confirmations after undo, conflicts, disabled preferences, deletion or enabled-set changes.
similar_patterns: describe_preference_reply_context() formats a confirmed application with ID, boundary and preference ID/text lines.
Agent _llm_context_lines() appends optional helper output only when non-empty.
Knowledge answers are deterministic and should remain sourced to data/path:line without changing retrieval.
Version metadata is synchronized through pyproject.toml, src/jarvis_lite/__init__.py and tests/test_project_metadata.py.
tests: tests/test_agent.py covers /ask, local data answers, memory fallback and injected fake LLM providers.
tests/test_llm.py covers provider instructions whitelist boundaries.
observation_report: The active confirmation matching rule is already centralized enough to reuse; duplicating it in Agent would increase drift risk.
The least invasive first-stage behavior is append-only audit formatting, not rewriting local answer content.
Local data answer and memory fallback are both Agent-owned return paths, so a small Agent formatter can keep the change scoped.
LLM provider instructions should remain unchanged because local answer formatting does not require LLM command routing.

key_questions.question: Which confirmation can format local answers?
key_questions.answer: Reuse the same active confirmation contract as ordinary LLM fallback: newest record only, status=confirmed, current enabled preference IDs/text exactly match the record, and no enabled preference conflicts exist.

key_questions.question: How should local knowledge and memory fallback be changed?
key_questions.answer: Append a short auditable preference formatting block when a valid confirmation exists. Do not alter data retrieval, match ordering, source lines, profile summary or route decisions.

key_questions.question: Should this add or expose new commands?
key_questions.answer: No. This phase only changes existing answer formatting. Preference management commands still stay out of LLM provider instructions.

key_questions.question: What invalidates the local answer note?
key_questions.answer: Undoing the confirmation, disabling/deleting a confirmed preference, enabling another preference after confirmation or creating a conflict makes the helper return an empty string.

acceptance: After enabling preferences and confirming them, a matching /ask local data answer includes a confirmed preference formatting audit note.
After undoing the confirmation, subsequent local data answers no longer include the audit note.
Long-term memory fallback includes the same audit note only while the confirmation is active.
Changing the enabled preference set after confirmation invalidates the audit note for local answers.
Provider instructions still do not list preference management commands or preference confirmation context text.
Version is bumped to 0.142.0 and docs/verification are updated.
